entry.title: Re: My new UDO does not appear in UDO's list in a UDF wit...
entry.content:
In the end, the solution was to remove the object and recreate it.

I don't have a clue why it didn't work the first time, but thankfully, B1 wa able to recover with a remove-recreate procedure.

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Perú - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to run the SAP DRC enabled legal reports for my country. However, the reports are not displaying information and not processing any information the templates. Below, I detail the procedures performed.

1) In the CBC, an organizational structure for data processing has already been created.

2) Activation of the Document and Report Compliance (5XU) scope element has been requested.

3) My user has all the necessary roles for the execution of legal reports.

BR_AP_MANAGER_PE

BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT_PE

BR_ADMINISTRATOR

4) In the app, the reports are displayed.

5) However, in this application it does not allow to run legal reports.

Please support any additional configuration that may be required. ?

that might be required to run SAP DRC legal reports ?

Thanks,

Antony

entry.title: Re: Export multiple datasphere objects at the same time a...
entry.content: The ER will not allow to restore the objects however, but via CLI one can download all objects

entry.title: Re: Notification when GR(Goods Receipt ) created within F...
entry.content:
Hi veeravani,

We have a business event (https://api.sap.com/event/CE_MATERIALDOCUMENTEVENTS/overview) that could be consumed in Event Mesh for example and then send a notification to the user.

You might need to check out Developer Extensibility and create an Event consumption model.

The other idea would be to check out Situation Handling https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/516c8ecb7462453ca430a42481cc33ec.html?locale=en-US

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Automatic Supplier Disqualification based on Certific...
entry.content: We have implemented the "Automated Supplier Disqualification" solution thru SAP CPI using Supplier Data API. Please have a try on this. If you need more details please connect with me "tamilselvan.sn@techmahindra.com".

entry.title: Re: FSV with local CoA not displaying in Balance sheet ap...
entry.content:
Hola Jeremy Deo

Please review the information to replicate the Hierarchy on this link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/a5f2a13c0e0f408d9481190784a49b13.html

Here is the SAP Note for Public Cloud that mentions the same solution:

3064950 - Financial Statement Version versus Hierarchy ID at Fiori reporting apps for Balance Sheet

https://me.sap.com/notes/3064950

 

I hope this information helps to fix your report

 

Best regards

Edna

entry.title: Re: Standard text in purchase order output management.
entry.content:
Hi aks778,

Could you be more precise with your requirement?

I am not sure about the Maintain Standard Texts getting text to the PO, but the Manage Text app allows you to define footers, etc to put on the master form.

Please see this previous community post

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/manage-texts-vs-manage-standard-texts/qaq-p/12812218

Which references this blog for using Maintain Standard Text with Sales Order output, perhaps you can check if the scenario applies for PO

https://blogs.sap.com/2022/01/31/s-4hana-cloud-managing-and-integrating-standard-text-in-sales-print-documents/

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: How to improve Date Dimension labels in a line chart from number value (202505) to name ('May')
entry.content: Hi Julia, when you activate the hierarchy in the data preview of the AM, do you see the descriptions? And are you using by chance hierarchy with directory (description is build differently there). Cheers, Martin

entry.title: Re: Issue on get data from ARIBA API Supplier Data with p...
entry.content: Looks like url and request body has some issue, did u first setup in postman and received response ?

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM LOSC creating Product WT instead of HU WT
entry.content: Did your issue got resolved, I am also facing same issue ?

entry.title: Re: Two different DM Custom View Plugins are mistakenly combined
Re: Multiple datasources for custom Adobe Form
entry.content:
Hi EgbertVenema,

Are you using the BTP Adobe forms service from Developer Extensibility?

Have you already checked the Developer Extensibility Print Forms?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/959664fed2114b7ba070c5bc8b4beba2.html?locale=en-US

Seems if you are developing an onstack app to do it, you should be able to pull the data you need?

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Two different DM Custom View Plugins are mistakenly c...
entry.content: Yes you can. There are two ways i can think of doing this. The first is the same as the original poster, create two independent plugins with different names , eg pluginProd & pluginDev. Or if you only want one plugin then add a switch into the configuration page then in the controller add logic into your code to execute either the production code or the dev code. I would recommend using 2 plugins otherwise when you deploy new dev code you will lose the production plugin as well until the app restarts.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere connection validation error with RFC_C...
entry.content: The issue was with the JDK I was using on my Cloud Connector.  Once I updated it the issue was resolved

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Creating a Logical Database in SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Had to check the calendar and date on this post to make sure we haven't fallen into some kind of time-bending hole.

I've started in SAP around 2005. Logical DBs have already been obsolete at that time. I know what these are but thankfully, never had to deal with one myself, as an ABAPer. Why on Earth would someone need instructions to create a new LDB in 2025? 😱 This is bonkers.

Readers, please never do this.

entry.title: Re: Purging data in SAP BW4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

Our requirement is to purge the data in more than 100 ADSO's based on retention period from business.

Few ADSO's have data in cold store, i tried to deleted the data in cold  store by selecting a single partition using " Deleted " option and tried to clean up  in Cockpit, 

however, the deleted partition  still  shows in DTO Admin screen. 

How to make it invisible in screen though the data is not shown in ADSO.

Thanks,

Shyamala.

entry.title: Re: Your account may be locked, inactive, or not included...
entry.content:
Hi @Prasann , hope you're doing great.

Have you checked below KBA?

3440428 - "Invalid Login" error when accessing SuccessFactors instance via Identity Authentication

This error can have multiple sources, so my suggestion for you is to create a document based on above KBA, and if this KBA does not help you with your issue, then share the document you've created with all the KBA steps and evidences with Product Support Team, and they will help you further.

Regards.

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Re: Transformation logic for SCD Type 2 in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hey there, may there be a misunderstanding? You are looking for a switch, but there is none. The SCD is just another name for time-dependency. Means you activate in your view date-from or date-to fields with semantic type: business date from / to.

DSP uses the semantic type: business date from / to then in AM via the variable: Reference Date Variable. If you do not use the AM variable, the current date is used by default. 

To load the data, just use the date-to field in the target table as key. When you consume the table, you use the semantic type. 

Hope this heps

Cheers,

Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP Databricks in SAP Business Data Cloud : Unifying SAP Business Data with Lakehouse Intelligen
entry.content: Glad you found it useful, @Adem_Baykal .

entry.title: Re: Add object to a transport request by program

entry.title: Re: The feasibility of two functionalities in Datasphere
entry.content:
1. Is it possible to sort by 2 measures on a single table?

No.

2. How to filter the rows where the corresponding measure value > 25%?

use Measure as dimension, then use the dimension as filter

entry.title: Re: SAP BDC Free Trial Version
entry.content:

I got answer like H2,2025.

entry.title: Re: Fields Removed from the Source... Now What?
entry.content: Even via CLI you can generate fields without restrictions. The "/" is due to the web based tool, nothing else. Like you have to cleansing values in TRFN while loading into IO in BW (#, !, ALL_CAPITAL...HEX). But correct, the best way is the intermediate view (same as creating extractors in R/3 where a intermediate view is always helpful)

entry.title: Error in Transformation Flow when using a remote table.
entry.content:
Hello,

In a Delta Transformation flow i'm uysing a view that select on a remote table. But for a given reason it complains. I've been searching for similar errors of doc that is not allowed but cant' find much (or I didn't search the right things). This is the error message.

The view "S4HANA.2VR_PR_PURCHASINGDOC" references the following remote tables (either directly or indirectly): "S4HANA.IMMPURCHASINGDOC"

If somebody could give me a hint ... thx, Harry

entry.title: Re: Best approach to define corporate colors in crystal r...
entry.content:
It sounds like you're dealing with a bit of a puzzle! Here are a few possibilities and steps you can take to identify how the corporate colors were embedded in your 2016 reports:

  1. Manual Embedding: It's possible that the colors were manually set for each chart or series. This would mean that each report was individually customized without a centralized color management system.

  2. Check for Formulas: Sometimes, colors can be set using formulas within Crystal Reports. Look for any formulas that might be setting colors using RGB values or predefined color names. You can find these in the formula workshop or by checking the properties of the chart elements 

    1
    .

     

  3. CRChart Configuration: Even though you mentioned not seeing any color-related macros, it's worth double-checking the CRChart configuration files or documentation. There might be settings or scripts that define the color palette.

  4. Centralized Color Management: If you want a more centralized solution moving forward, consider creating a shared formula or a parameter that defines the color palette. This way, you can update the colors in one place, and all reports using this shared resource will reflect the changes.

  5. Consult Documentation or Support: If you're still having trouble, consulting the documentation for Crystal Reports or reaching out to their support might provide more insights. There might be features or best practices for managing color palettes that aren't immediately obvious.

Would you like more detailed steps on any of these suggestions?

entry.title: 🔄 Mapping Pricing Conditions from SAP CPS to SAP CPQ and Back to S/4HANA Sales Documents
entry.content:
This blog explores how to map pricing conditions from CPS to CPQ, and how to pass them back to S/4HANA Sales Order, Solution Quotation, or Solution Order—ensuring consistency, accuracy, and automation.

Note : Ensure you have Document Pricing is enabled in your SAP CPQ tenant

Understand the difference between Stalesless Pricing vs Document Pricing from the ground level.

In the evolving SAP ecosystem, integrating pricing logic across systems like SAP CPS (Configuration & Pricing Services), SAP CPQ (Configure Price Quote), and S/4HANA is critical for delivering a seamless quote-to-cash process. 

How to set Pricing Procedures to populate the Prices coming from CPS to CPQ and CPQ to backend S/4 HANA

How to map CPS Pricing Condition Types to the SAP CPQ Quote Line Items Fields

🧩 Why Map Pricing Conditions to SAP CPQ Line Items?

SAP CPQ acts as the central quoting engine in many enterprise sales processes. However, pricing logic often resides in Variant Pricing (VP) and Pricing Procedures defined in S/4HANA or CPS. Mapping these conditions to CPQ line items is essential because:

  • Consistency: Ensures pricing in CPQ matches backend systems.
  • Automation: Reduces manual intervention and errors.
  • Transparency: Sales reps see the same pricing logic as backend users.
  • Integration: Enables seamless handover of quotes to S/4HANA for order processing.

 

🔧 Key Components Involved

  1. CPS (Configuration & Pricing Services),– Hosts the pricing logic and variant configuration and pricing.
  2. SAP CPQ – Front-end for sales reps to configure and quote with Document Pricing logic via CPS.
  3. S/4HANA – Backend system for order processing and fulfillment.
  4. Pricing Procedure – Defines how pricing is calculated using condition types.
  5. Condition Types – Represent discounts, surcharges, taxes, etc.
  6. Variant Configuration (VC) – Drives product configuration and stateless pricing logic.
  7. Standard Integrations - SAP provides standard integration packages for placing a order to S/4 HANA Public, Private Cloud and Onprem

 

🛠️ Steps to Map Pricing Conditions from CPS to CPQ

1. Define Pricing Conditions in S/4HANA

  • Create or reuse condition types (e.g., PR00, K004, RA01).
  • Assign them to a pricing procedure.
  • Ensure access sequences are defined for condition records.

2. Expose Pricing Logic via CPS

  • CPS acts as a middleware to expose S/4HANA pricing logic.
  • Use APIs or integration flows to expose condition types and pricing results.

3. Configure SAP CPQ to Consume CPS Pricing

  • Enable SAP Variant Configuration & Pricing (CPS) integration in CPQ.
  • Ensure you have Document Pricing enabled in your CPQ for interaction (interactive Sales Pricing)
  • Map CPS pricing outputs to CPQ line item fields via Setup - Pricing - Pricing Procedures.
  • For custom business requirement, Use scripting or custom logic to parse CPS response and populate CPQ QICF.

4. Map Condition Types to CPQ Line Items

  • Use Standard Quote Item fields or Create Quote Item custom fields in CPQ to hold condition values (e.g., base price, discounts, customer price, Rebates, Surcharges, Free Item.... etc).
  • Use mapping logic to assign CPS condition types to these fields.
  • Ensure the mapping supports multiple condition types per line item.

5. Pass Pricing Back to S/4HANA via Standard Integration Packages

  • When quote is accepted by customer, CPQ sends the configuration and pricing data back via Integration Iflow.
  • Iflow is mapped with all Pricing conditions set in CPQ ( Pricing Procedure Mapping)
  • Iflow will use standard APIs to create:
    • Sales Orders
    • Solution Quotation
    • Solution Order
  • Ensure pricing conditions are passed as part of the payload or re-determined in S/4HANA using the same pricing procedure.

Below is the Integration mapped from SAP CPQ Quote Item fields to SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud - Solution Order

Below is the Integration mapped from SAP CPQ Quote Item fields to SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud - Solution Quotation

🔄 Round-Trip Pricing Flow

✅ Best Practices

  • Use consistent condition types across systems.
  • Test pricing logic in CPS thoroughly before exposing to CPQ.
  • Use version control for pricing procedures and VC models.
  • Log and monitor CPS calls for performance and accuracy.
  • Train sales teams on how pricing is derived in CPQ.

📌 Conclusion

Mapping pricing conditions from CPS to CPQ and back to S/4HANA is a foundational step in achieving a unified and automated sales process. By aligning pricing logic across systems, businesses can ensure accuracy, reduce errors, and accelerate the quote-to-cash cycle.

entry.title: SAP PI - Group records based on values of the source message
entry.content:
Hi all,

Ive an requirement to group and filter based on the source values:

Ive the following nested Stucture that needs to be mapped/grouped and filtered to the target nested structure:

The Source message can be like this:

And the target XML should look like this:

  • When there is only TestId, the TestId and its Parameter should be passed.
  • When there is both SampleId and TestId, only SampleId should be passed and all the corresponding Parameters of that SampleId should be grouped on. In this case 3 parameters for SampleId (S1) and 1 Parameter for SampleId (S2).
  • Also when there is no TargetId field, the corresponding parameters shouldnt be passed (like parameter P3).

My preference is graphical mapping...
I would be appreciated if you can help me with this, 

Thank you,
John

entry.title: Re: ME32K/ME31K - Badi/ User exit

so the fields are not in the user exit.

entry.title: Launchpad News Tile
entry.content:
Hi community,

I'm looking for experience with the News Tile in the new Fiori Launchpad My Home page. Does anyone have experience with creating their own news tile and filling it with their own feed?

So far we've managed to use known RSS feeds for this, but nothing of our own yet and SAP doesn't seem to have an app for it?


I look forward to hearing about your experiences with it.

regards

Lars

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud SAP weblink

Hi @pradeepchillakuru ,

In my blog From A to Z: Setup a Starter System of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition [2024 Edition], I discuss all the details when you use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for the first time.

Regards,

George

entry.title: 🌟 Join Us Live: Q&A Session with SAP Sales Cloud V2 Experts!
entry.content:
Are you working with SAP Sales Cloud V2 and have questions about how to make the most of its features? Whether you're just getting started or already have some experience under your belt, this Live Q&A Session is your chance to connect directly with our product experts and get the answers you need.

📅 Event Details:

  • Date: June 11, 2025

  • Time: 7:00 AM PST

  • Duration: 60 minutes

  • Speakers: Nikhil Betigeri & Adithya Uday

  • Audience: Business Users & Consultants (Beginner Level)

  • Registration: Click here to save your spot! This session will not be recorded, so don’t miss it!

     

💡 Why You Should Attend

This interactive session is designed to give you real-time access to insights from SAP Sales Cloud V2 specialists. Our experts will address:

  • Common challenges and misunderstandings users face

  • Practical guidance on how to better use key features

  • Answers to your product-related questions during a live "Ask Me Anything" (AMA) format

Whether you're troubleshooting a specific issue or looking to deepen your understanding of the platform, this is a great opportunity to gain clarity and confidence in your use of SAP Sales Cloud V2.

🎯 Learning Objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

  • Recognize and avoid common mistakes when using SAP Sales Cloud V2

  • Get direct answers to your product functionality questions

  • Interact with experts in a live, supportive environment

✉️ Don’t Forget to Bring Your Questions!

This is your time to connect, learn, and unlock the full potential of SAP Sales Cloud V2. We encourage you to come prepared with questions—our experts are here to help!

Secure your spot and join us on June 11th for an insightful and interactive session!

entry.title: Changing field from NUMC6 to dec 2
entry.content:
Hello Community
I changed a table field with domain numc6 to decimal type with size 6 and decimal 2:

I ran Se14 preserving the data and transported it to Quality .Now when I go to se14 in quality I get the message:

If i click in 'Continue adjustment' button then  i receive dump :

Does anyone know how to resolve the error without losing table data?

entry.title: Re: EWM Outbound delivery - how to update a custom status value?
entry.content:
Thanks, everyone, for the replies here, as well as on other websites. Eventually, I had to come to the conclusion that this is simply not feasible. I've tried everything mentioned here and everything "where used" or even random search could find. Spent a hellish day in debugger. But every clue was a dead-end that would just straight-up get rejected or do nothing or be a slap in the face in some other way. I suspect these statuses are just not meant to be used exactly as we envisioned.

In the end, we just ran out of time and went with the worst possible option of a direct DB update. (No my choice obvs.) I don't see an option to close the question with no answer, so will have to leave it hanging, I guess.

FYI @Daniil , thanks again!

entry.title: CAPM email issue : Concurrent connections limit exceeded SAP cloud SDK mail-client
entry.content:
i have to send email via CAPM for which I have used @SAP-cloud-sdk/mail-client.

The requirement is such that i need to send emails to diffrent recipeints in loop, the scenario is there is a fiori list report in which I select rows and click on Send mail button created as extension custom action for calling below code. this works fine for few emails but throws connection execceded error randomly for more number of rows.

email-handler.js

============

 await    sendMail({ destinationName: emailDestinationName }, [mailConfig], {
                            sdkOptions: {
                                parallel: false
                            }
                        }).then(() => {
                            console.log('Emails sent successfully.');
                        }).catch((err) => {
                            console.error('Failed to send emails:', err);
                        });

Failed to send emails: Error: Message failed: 432 4.3.2 Concurrent connections limit exceeded. Visit https://aka.ms/concurrent_sending

How to handle concurrent connections. Please help.

entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
@TomHoepping @julieplummer20 

Based on the report from Tom earlier today, did a quick check on M3 Mac, macOS 15.4.1 (Sequoia), Docker Desktop 4.41.2, Docker Engine 28.1.1... and.... it works just fine. So that's great news. 

[Just noticed, that macOS 15.5 is available, so let's see once macOS is upgraded if everything still works... 😱 ... upgrade done, container restarted ok and can login fine, so seems like macOS 15.5 is also good for using this container].

sudo docker run --stop-timeout 3600 -it --name a4h -h vhcala4hci -p 3200:3200 -p 3300:3300 -p 3601:3601 -p 8443:8443 -p 30213:30213 -p 50000:50000 -p 50001:50001 sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 -skip-limits-check -agree-to-sap-license 

Julie, you could adjust the advice at https://hub.docker.com/r/sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial#macos to reflect the fact that macOS and Docker Desktop path is now usable again even for arm64 MacBooks.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Intent Based Navigation
entry.content: After much trial and error, I found out that the method  adaptNavigationParameterExtension can be used in the controller extension to capture the intend-based navigation.

entry.title: Re: Job Profile
entry.content:
Hi @Anjali255 ,

Navigate to Manage Data:

In the Admin Center, go to Manage Data.

Search for the Job Template:

Use the search bar to find the Job Template associated with your Job Profile Template. You can search by the template's name or external code.

Access the Job Template Details:

Click on the relevant Job Template to open its details.

Locate the Section:

Scroll down to the shortDescription or longDescription sections. These correspond to the content sections in your Job Profile Template.

Go to Configure Object Definition.

Search for Job Template Section.

Take action and enable the internalId field as "Read Only".

Return to Manage Data and open the Job Template again. The internalId will now be displayed. This is the internal identifier for the section.

Determine the External Code:

The external Code for the section is typically the same as the internalId. However, if it's different, you can find it by:

Navigating to Manage Job Profile Template.

Selecting the relevant template.

Viewing the section details to find the external code.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Invalid Internal Candidate Record Created in RCM-Can'...
entry.content:
Hello All,

I'm responding to my original community question.  We were able to work with SAP to confirm the root cause.  I know other customers have experienced this issue and hopefully this information helps you.

  • We did confirm that the root cause of this issue in Release/Preview is due to an SAP Gigya migration referenced in this KBA
  • The Gigya migration was universally pushed to all preview instances and requires a unique and valid email address for all candidates.
  • In our system, the Internal Candidate profile is created at the completion of the Post Hire Verification step in Onboarding 1.0.  In order to avoid this issue, when going through the post hire validation step in, the recommendation is to put a new unique email or add a (.) to an email address so for example if the candidate’s email was Tylawater@gmail.com, instead make it Tyla.water@gmail.com
  • Gigya will NOT be universally pushed to production, this will be admin opt in. This means that the issue will not occur in PROD and external hiring will not be impacted.

SAP is working on a better solution for the preview instance.  In the meantime, prior to the better solution, the advice is to update your testing scripts so that you enter a new unique email address in the post hire validation step of Onboarding 1.0 as indicated above.

Juanita

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Cloud Integration to Consume/Connect to Datasphere OData API with OAuth 2.0
entry.content: @Ali_Chalhoub , can you please provide correct link?

entry.title: Re: gender field becomes empty when attaching a resume
entry.content:
Hi @user116 ,

Ensure that the gender field is correctly defined in your application template:

Standard Gender Field: If you're using the standard gender field (gender), it typically supports only the values 'No Selection', 'Male', and 'Female'. Custom values are not supported unless you create a new custom field.


Custom Gender Field: If you've created a custom gender field (e.g., cust_gender), verify that it's properly configured in the application template XML. Ensure that the picklist-id corresponds to the correct picklist and that the field is marked as custom="true".

Field Permissions in Application Template: Permissions are applied top to bottom in the XML template. Ensure that the gender field has appropriate read/write permissions set for the relevant recruiting operators.

Multistage Applications: If you're using multistage applications, field permissions for recruiting operator roles (except for resume, coverLetter, and statusId fields) must be defined in the job requisition template.

If you're using a custom picklist for the gender field:

Picklist Values: Ensure that the picklist includes all desired values (e.g., 'Male', 'Female', 'Prefer not to answer') and that these values are correctly mapped in the field definition.

Picklist ID: Verify that the picklist-id in the field definition matches the ID of the picklist you're using.

Hope this helps!! Let me know how it goes.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: SAP SEGW vs RAP vs CAP: Making the most of Function Imports, Actions and Functions (Part 1)
entry.content:
Interesting subject, thanks for sharing!

I haven't seen function import abused out in the wild, to be honest. If anything, it's not well-known and I've seen a few cases where it's the opposite: what really should be a function import is clumsily wrapped in the regular CRUD methods.

IMHO in SEGW function import is awesome sauce and I didn't like RAP actions by comparison. RAP's "no commit" principle tends to get in the way too (business users just want stuff happen magically). Looking forward to the next installment.

entry.title: Re: Physical inventory in EWM
entry.content: Make sure all the number ranges are correctly assigned including number ranges of PI documents as well as  Warehouse Order.

entry.title: Re: SAP Databricks in SAP Business Data Cloud : Unifying SAP Business Data with Lakehouse Intelligen
entry.content: Look forward to any hints at coordination of BDC Catalog, Unity Catalog and Collibra...

entry.title: Re: Job Profile
entry.content: Thank You Karthik, I have gone through the steps as you mentioned above, but unfortunately there is no external ID associated for the section so i am unable to upload the long description for the section, System throwing the error : ExternalCode is a required field/association in Object JobProfileLocalizedData.

entry.title: Re: Issues Downloading Producer Inside Client's Citrix
entry.content: Hi Shane. Thanks for the info but we aren't using a shortcut. We are getting this error in our customer's Citrix when clicking Producer from the Manager. I can't even get this far when I click Producer from the Manager. I get a connection error once I click Producer. We do not have any issues outside the Citrix.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Data visualization with dynamic column by using the ALV report
entry.content:
Thanks, ChatGPT! 🙄

There is nothing "dynamic" in this example though.

It has a SELECT with specific fields, then creates an ALV field catalog with the same fields, and then for some reason uses the old container ALV class. And the result doesn't even have field labels.

Field catalog in ALV is always "dynamic", by definition. What is shown here is boilerplate ALV functionality but with extra steps that add no value because they can't be used in any truly "dynamic" way. The very first use case says "In scenarios where users need to select fields to display". Well, how would you use this code example to accomplish something like that?

There are plenty of SALV demo reports in SAP for the boilerpate stuff. For something truly dynamic, I'd guess you'll need dynamic SELECT as well as RTTS. I really don't see any value in the code example shared here, sorry. But maybe I'm missing something...

entry.title: Nota credito parcial y total de una misma factura
entry.content:
Hola Expertos

¿Quisiera saber por qué el sistema permite hacer una NC parcial y luego una NC total de la factura y duplica el inventario con la NC parcial creada inicialmente ?

El escenario es el siguiente 

Se realizó la devolución de 1000 unidades de una posición afectando a la factura N° No.xxx

NC No.xxx 1000 unidades 

Posterior el cliente indicó que requería que se efectuara la NC por el total facturado y se emita una nueva factura solo por la cantidad que recibió

Si procede a realizar una Nota de Débito 

Nota Debito N°xxx cruza valor con la Nota Credito N°xxx 1000 unidades

Luego se realizó nuevo pedido y entrega contabilizada para generar la NC por el total de la factura

Al realizar el nuevo pedido y entrega por el total de la factura se vuelve a retornar las 1000 unidades de la posición por lo que se duplicó la cantidad devuelta. 

El sistema permitió generar la devolución del total a pesar de que ya se había efectuado una NC por la 1000 unidades.

entry.title: Re: EWM Outbound delivery - how to update a custom status value?
entry.content: Maybe this help page will point you to an option different from the direct DB update? https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/sewm/3354622098.html#EWMdeliverystatushandling-HowstatusesarecalculatedinEWM

entry.title: EKKO Table RLWRT Field
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I have a case that a Purchase order is with one value and has been aproved.

But, in EKKO, the RLWRT Field is with a complete different and smaller value.

And, i have a Z transaction that uses RLTWRT to some processes. 

Anybody has some ideia of why the value in EKKO is not correct ?

Thank you all

entry.title: Re: OData V4 code based implementation - Overview
entry.content:
Hello andre.fischer

We need to develop an OData service to support a non-SAP frontend, with data strictly in JSON format. The service should implement full CRUD operations, along with support for file and image uploads.

Could you please advise whether it would be more appropriate to use code-based OData V4, or if we should stick with V2 for this scenario?

Best Regards,

Smriti

entry.title: Re: taxReclaimCode is required for domestic taxes
entry.content:
Hey Gábor,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

This error  “taxReclaimCode is required for domestic taxes” usually pops up when SAP expects a tax reclaim code for the expense line, even if the G/L account itself isn’t tax-relevant.

Even though the G/L account doesn’t deal with taxes directly, the tax code used in the expense report might still be set up to allow input tax deduction (basically, it thinks you're trying to reclaim VAT).

When that happens, SAP wants to know exactly how you're reclaiming the tax and that’s where the taxReclaimCode comes in.

How to fix it:

You’ve got a couple of options, depending on whether the tax should be reclaimed or not:

If you're not reclaiming tax:

  • Double-check the tax code on the expense line.
  • Make sure it’s one that doesn’t allow VAT reclaim. If it is, switch to a tax-exempt one or remove it if it’s not needed.

If you do want to reclaim tax:

  • Then you need to send a valid taxReclaimCode from Concur.
  • This usually requires updating the configuration in Concur (or the middleware like CPI if you're using it) to pass that code along with the expense data.

I hope those elements will answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: MFA setup for SAP PO/PI risks
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are planning to implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for logging into our SAP PO/PI systems. We have around 230+ partner systems connected to our SAP backend via SAP PO/PI. Could you please let me know if there are any risks associated with this, particularly regarding potential impacts on connectivity with these partner systems?

Regards, 

Ramu.

entry.title: Re: Transformation logic for SCD Type 2 in Datasphere
entry.content: OK I see, that makes sense. Actuall I have already done that using the method you suggested above but I was confused that may be there is any transformation or configuration. Anyways thanks for that. Regards

entry.title: Re: Job Profile
entry.content:
Hi @Anjali255 ,

I understand you're encountering an error stating that the ExternalCode is a required field when attempting to upload the long description for a new section in the Job Profile Template within SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management (RCM).

This issue arises because the JobProfileLocalizedData object requires an ExternalCode to associate the localized content with a specific section.


Identify the Section ID (ExternalCode):

  • Navigate to Admin Center > Manage Data.
  • Search for Job Template by specifying the external code.
  • Locate the specific Job Template you're interested in and click on the Details link.
  • Scroll down to the section you're interested in. The internalId displayed here is the Section ID.

If the internalId is not visible, enable it by going to Configure Object Definition, searching for Job Template Section, and setting the internalId field visibility to "Read Only".

  • Create the JobProfileLocalizedData Entry:

In Manage Data, create a new entry for the JobProfileLocalizedData object.

Fill in the required fields:

externalCode: Use the externalCode of the Job Template.

locale: Specify the locale (e.g., en_US for English - United States).

sectionId: Use the internalId (Section ID) identified earlier.

sectionType: Specify the type of the section (e.g., JobDescription).

shortDescription: Provide a brief description.

longDescription: Provide the detailed description.

Save and Validate:

Save the new JobProfileLocalizedData entry.

Ensure that the data is correctly associated and that there are no validation errors.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Account Receivable support, Alternative account or not
entry.content:
Hi Chanaporn,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Accounts Receivable does not typically support manual selection of an alternative account directly within the "Create Outgoing Invoice" app. The system usually determines the customer reconciliation account automatically, based on:

  • The customer master data (under the company code segment), and
  • The account determination configuration for revenue postings.

 

If you're looking for an alternative account (like a different AR G/L account), here’s how SAP usually handles it:

  • Alternative G/L accounts aren’t selectable manually in the invoice app.
  • Instead, you’d have to:
    • Either assign a different reconciliation account in the customer master (if applicable),
    • Or handle it through substitution logic or enhancement (which is limited in Public Cloud).

 

Tip:

If you're trying to post to a different G/L account for a particular case (like intercompany or special scenarios), you might want to look into:

  • Manual journal entries for adjustments, or
  • Event-Based Revenue Recognition or manual clearing processes, depending on the context.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Stated Amount Including Input Tax - Manage service entry sheet - lean service app
entry.content:
Hello Baher.

I have the same requirement and after some researches I've realized that this field will contain only values of non-deductible taxes. My customer still want to see deductible taxes values summed in this field.

Did you solved your problem with any development?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Filter Variable on Fields with Compound Keys
entry.content:
Hi, let's say you have in you model-view (FACT-view) G/L Account and Chart Of Accounts fields. Compounded keys.

In the AM you create one Filter Variable for G/L Account, the AM will filter the selected G/L Account/s regardless of Chart of Accounts. 

In the AM you create second Variable for the Chart of Accounts, AM will filter all G/L Accounts belonging to the one Chart of Account. 

If you use both variables, you will get the expected result. Means compounded key as you know from BW is not used in DSP. 

Even if you use parameters passed from model-view passed to AM. Same for associations.

entry.title: Reforma Tributária, 2026 Valor total da NF
entry.content:
Depois de aplicar as notas de SD para RVABRA (3556404) durante os teste percebemos que o IBS e CBS estão somando ao total da NF, mas neste primeiro ano entende-se que o total da NF não seria alterado, concordam? em 2027 teoricamente CBS IBS alterariam os valores, mas em 2026 não?

Eu abri um ticket para produto para consulta e saber se a SAP vai liberar nota ou devemos achar um ponto e fazer Z o desenvolvimento?

Qual é o entendimento que tem sobre este ponto?

Abraço.

entry.title: Log de errores contabilización de stock QM
entry.content:
Buen dia a todos.

Tengo la siguiente novedad al momento de liberar en QM.

Se ha revisado lo siguiente:

  1. Roles.
  2. Parametros.
  3. Objetos.

Todo esta completamente igual, pero la cuestion es que un usuario que tiene lo mismo roles, parametros y objetos si puede liberar:

El mensaje es el siguiente:

Línea de log:
AQUI VA EL NOMBRE DEL USUARIO 13.05.2025 14:42:58
26.088 UN en libre defectuoso, mensaje error:
El status del sistema **CTEC** está activo (ORD 103001656)
ID mensaje: **BS 13**

Muchas gracias.

entry.title: Re: ME32K/ME31K - Badi/ User exit
entry.content: Oh okay, I understand now. You have used the Custom Fields and Logic Fiori app to add the fields. I can't help on that.

entry.title: Re: Changing field from NUMC6 to dec 2
entry.content: REASON: the problem is that for instance 314314 cannot be stored in DEC 6.2, as its biggest possible value is 9999.99, you should have defined DEC 9.2 because the first digit must be odd as per PACK logic to store a value up to 9999999.99. If you want to store 314314 as 3143.14, you must create a conversion program.

entry.title: Re: Changing field from NUMC6 to dec 2
entry.content: You may ask the administrator to do a backup of the QCM<yourtable> table, it's the original table, just in case you do an error.

entry.title: Re: SAP Workzone Local Entry Point plan availability
entry.content: Hi Ryan, I have never seen this screen. Is that available for SAP Customers or SAP Partners? Where would I find it? Or do you share SAP internal information in a public forum?

entry.title: Re: Changing field from NUMC6 to dec 2
entry.content:
For information, the database adjustment renames the table <table> into QCM<table>, creates the table QCM8<table> with the new definition, copies the data from QCM<table> to QCM8<table> with conversion for the changed columns, and then renames QCM8<table> into <table> (if everything is fine).

I advise rollbacking the adjustment. To do this, see the procedure https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_crm700_ehp01/7.0.1.15/en-US/cf/21f1c5446011d189700000e8322d00/frameset.htm or in the note https://me.sap.com/notes/1925322/E (delete QCM8 and so on)

entry.title: ECP - Generate 2 W2 in a year and 2 paystubs in a pay period - without using concurrent employment
entry.content:
Hi Team, As per below requirement, since ECP needs to Generate 2 W2 in a year and 2 paystubs in a pay period, it looks like having two distinct employments is the only option. Which means we will have to enable concurrent employment. Is there a way to solve this without using concurrent employment?

Scenario: Gov. License is issued to employees, for specific Legal entities. Sometimes such employees may work across two different regions that are managed by 2 distinct legal entities. As a result, in case of a legal case, Audit process requires paystub & W2 tax form to show that the employee was paid out of two distinct legal entities. This is to ensure that the legal case doesn’t pull multiple legal entities when the case is being filed against one of the 2 LE. Also, Paystub and W2 are the credible documents that Legal requires to prove that employee worked for a specific LE on certain dates [paystub will contain entries for each date]

Additional requirements:

1. Employees can move between 2 legal entities back and forth within the same pay period.

2. Volume is less than 100 employees in a year that move across two legal entities on ad-hoc basis.

 

 

Other components of the solution: EC, RCM, ONB. Time solution is a non-SAP legacy solution but will be replaced in the future with new one in few years.

entry.title: RUNLOGIC_PH occasionally throws CX_SY_CONVERSION_NO_NUMBER The argument '*000' cannot be interpreted
entry.content:
Hi Community, 

Running BPC Standard for our consolidation solution.  

BPC4HANA 200 08 on BW4HANA 200 SP9

We're using RUNLOGIC_PH to process a script in parallel threads.  It works great, however, occasionally, in the package log we get:

An exception with the type CX_SY_CONVERSION_NO_NUMBER was raised, but was not handled locally or declared in a RAISING clause.

The argument '*000' cannot be interpreted as a number

No formula log is generated.  When we rerun it doesn't happen and we get the usual formula log listing the characteristic members and corresponding threads starting/stopping. 

To give an idea of how intermittently this happens, since implementing RUNLOGIC_PH in September 2023, we've seen this error in April 2024, January 2025 & March 2025.  Since one of these was during consolidation of Q1 and another during consolidation of year end we'd like to avoid as it delays the consolidation process while being rerun. 

Any solution, discussion, commiseration are welcome. 

Thanks,

Wes

entry.title: Missing Data in UPL

entry.title: BPC EPM Dynamic Selection for Actual/Budget/Current Estimate in YTD and Annual Format
entry.content:
I've created a report(below) in BPC EPM that shows two main sections:

1) YTD comparing Current YTD Actuals/Current YTD Plan/YTD Prior Year Actuals

2) Annual Total Current Estimate (updated Monthly)/Current Plan/Prev Year Actuals

The columns in Green are excel formulas and grey column headers are direct from BPC. 

The report did what we needed for this past month end and saved time, as the old method was to run the report multiple times-once each for Actuals, Plan and Current Estimate. However, for next month end I'm going to have to Edit the report and swap out the April Current Estimate for the May Current Estimate. I'll also have to update the YTD formulas as they just add up hidden monthly columns. My ideal is to have Dynamic Selections/Variables at the Top of the report where the user would enter:

YTD Selections:

Period Range - Which would control the YTD section for Time. ie Current Month=May, so YTD is 2025.01 - 2025.05.

Plan Version - Can be selected. The Actuals Columns could remain "Hardcoded"

Annual/Total Year Selections:

Current Year- Can be selected. The Previous Year selection could be offset based on this.

Plan and Current Estimate - Can be selected. Actual column probably remains hardcoded.

I have a background in FICO and creating Finance queries in BW Query Designer. I'm confident I could build the report above by using BW Query Designer connected to the BPC infoprovider/"cube"... but my Manager wants it in BPC. I'm new to BPC and i definitely don't have the Dynamic Selections thing figured out yet. Please let me know if you have specific suggestions or point me toward documentation on setting up Dynamic Selections/Variables. I've watched some Youtube videos on BPC, but so far they are fairly basic.

Thanks in advance for any help 🙂

entry.title: Re: Enable Now - Publishing as SCORM 1.2 to Docebo (Folde...
entry.content:
Hi Andrew,

You typically do load content into an LMS with a Book object as the parent object of the content structure, however, you can use a Group object if you like.  One thing to consider when doing this is if you want to track the completion of the content, only Book objects and Simulation Mode objects, e.g. Demo Mode, act as SCOs to report back completion status.  In other words, you can use a Group object as the parent object but will then not be able to report completion of the content.

The only options that I can think of to hide the folder structure, assuming that's what you loaded are:

1. Hide the second level folder objects.  However, if you do that users will require some way to navigate from the top of the structure through to lower levels
2. When publishing the content make sure that you set the following setting

 

 

 

 

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Bem vindos a Comunidade SAP de Londrina!
entry.content: Parabéns pelo iniciativa 👏🏿!

entry.title: Relacja ze spotkania Stammtisch 23.04.2025. Dyskrecja obowiązuje, chyba że historia jest zbyt dobra!
entry.content:
Witajcie,

kolejne spotkanie za nami. Pewnie już zauważyliście, że organizujemy spotkania trochę żadziej niż w zeszłym roku ale za to dokładamy na spotkaniach jeszcze więcej treści. Na tym spotkaniu odwiedził nas kolega z Krakowa. Rozmowa o pracy przerodziła się w rozmowę o współpracy i wystartowania kolejnego Stammtisch'u w Krakowie. A skoro Stammtisch się rozwija to właśnie skąd ta nazwa i co w niej takiego co łączy ją z SAP'em?

Stammtisch to słówko z niemieckiego, które składa się z dwóch części:

  • „Stamm” — oznacza plemię, ród, albo stały, czyli coś, co się nie zmienia, jak twoja ekipa od lat.

  • „Tisch” — wiadomo, stół.

Czyli dosłownie: stół dla stałej ekipy.

W dawnych czasach — w niemieckich gospodach (czyli Gaststätten) — był to dosłownie zarezerwowany stół dla stałych bywalców. Często nad stołem wisiała nawet tabliczka z napisem „Stammtisch”, żeby nikt przypadkiem tam nie usiadł. Taki stół miał też swój niepisany regulamin i własny klimat. Tam się omawiało lokalne plotki, politykę, mecze Bayernu, a czasem nawet wielkie filozoficzne kwestie, jak np. „czy to ostatnie piwo to już za dużo, czy jeszcze nie?”.

W Niemczech Stammtisch do dziś jest żywą tradycją — nawet w wersji współczesnej. Może to być cotygodniowe spotkanie znajomych, klubu wędkarskiego albo fanów planszówek. W czasach internetu powstały nawet „Online-Stammtische”, czyli wideoczaty dla ludzi, którzy chcą pogadać jak przy starym stole, ale przez kamerkę.

I jeszcze ciekawostka:
Jeśli kiedykolwiek pójdziesz do niemieckiej knajpy i zobaczysz stół z tabliczką „Stammtisch” — nie siadaj tam bez pytania! To nie jest zwykły stół. To świętość. Prawie jak tron Odyna w Valhalli.

 A nasz Stammtisch w Katowicach? To taki stolik VIP-ów w knajpie, ale nie takich z limuzynami i drogimi zegarkami, tylko raczej lokalnych bohaterów Technologii SAP i służbowych bywalców tej technologii. To miejsce w pubie, barze, na lokalnej plaży albo kawiarni, przy którym zawsze siedzi ta sama ekipa (+/-), gadają o życiu spędzanym w SAP, czasem się skrzeczają, czasem śmieją — generalnie rozwiązują problemy SAPa i świata przy piwie albo kawie. Pełna kultura i zasady trochę jak na forum internetowe, tylko na żywo i z kuflem w ręku lub filiżanką 😄. 

Wracając na chwilę do naszego spotkania to mówiąc krótko spotkaliśmy się w przemiłymi ludźmi rozawiajać jak zawsze o sprawach SAP'owych.

 

Mamy bardzo dobre wieści też jeśli chodzi o najbliższe tygodnie. Jedna z lokalnych firm organizuje corocznego What's Upa! Szczegóły w linku: 

https://accenture.higher.pl/whats-up-SAP-event

Przy okazji nie zapomnij się zapisać na kolejnego Stammtischa. Szczegóły tutaj: 
Katowice - SAP Stammtisch 02.07.2025 - SAP Community

Są to niepowtarzalne okazjie na zdobycie kolejnych dobrych doświadczeń i wymienienia się wiedzą. Nienamawiamy tylko zapraszamy :-)!

Zarezerwuj już teraz następny termin w swoim kalendarzu:

  • 2 lipca 2025

     



    Pozdrawiamy serdecznie, do zobaczenia 👋😊 , 🍻

entry.title: Re: SAP Databricks in SAP Business Data Cloud : Unifying SAP Business Data with Lakehouse Intelligen
entry.content: @glenleslie This is a good blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-business-data-cloud-central-catalog-feature-revealed/ba-p/14097009 that talks about the BDC catalog and Collibra integration. As far as Unity is concerned from BDC perspective, its an operational catalog which is useful for the personas (data scientist, data analyst, ML Engineer etc) working within SAP Databricks so that they don't have to leave their natural habitat so to speak. Will release a blog talking about this in near future.

entry.title: Re: "Revolutionizing SAP Implementations: How AI is Chang...
entry.content:
Great Ideas Chaitanya G. and thanks for sharing. Having Chat GPT and Copilot of the world that could be fed with Business requirement with appropriate prompting eg. List of the Business Process, Map it to SAP S4H, List the Gaps that may require to build code, Get the Design, Get the Test Script etc in PPT, Word, Excel...cutting all manual task with improved productivity. Of Course, they are not ready to be consumed directly and must be aligned and validated with all the Business and IT stakeholders...but is a good starting point without reinventing the wheel.

Could you please share more which SAP Tool can help here with the cost and time line deploy, as organizations will rather prefer market std SAP's tool from the perspective of reliability and confidentiality, then using other GPTs product floating in the market. Thank you again!!

Saket Chourasia

entry.title: REST API direct integration don't open for alter
entry.content: I'm trying to open the REST API within the integrations but it doesn't open when I click on any of them What happened?

entry.title: Restriction of permissions for notifications with code group "OP-Change" in transactions IW21, IW22
entry.content:
1. Introduction
As part of the internal change management process at Schluchseewerk in the context of S/4HANA, the functionality "Notifications" in the SAP module EAM is utilized. For the operational implementation of this process, we have planned the use of OP notifications. A permission concept has been created for these and partially implemented technically.

During the implementation, an individual role Z_EAM_NOTIFICATION_OP was created, tailored to specific permissions within the notification processing framework.

2. Justification
To ensure a clean and controlled change management process, it is imperative that only authorized users with specific roles have access to OP notifications. In particular, it must be prevented that users without the appropriate authorization can:
- create (transaction IW21),
- View (transaction IW23), or
- change (transaction IW22) OP notifications with the code group (QMGRP) "OP-Change."

Adhering to this restriction is central to quality assurance, traceability of changes, and minimizing potential errors in the change management process.

3. Objective
The goal is to adjust the permissions in SAP so that notifications with the code group OP-Change can only be created, viewed, or changed by authorized individuals. For users with the appropriate permissions for the role, the following restrictions should apply:
- The code group OP-Change should not be visible in the catalog selection,
- it should not be used for creating notifications,
- existing notifications of this group should neither be displayed nor edited unless the user has the appropriate permissions.

4. Technical Issue
In the course of the previous permission check, the authorization object Q_GP_CODE – Use of group codes was already employed. This made it possible to hide the code group OP-Change in the catalog selection and thus prevent the creation of corresponding notifications. However, it remains technically possible to display or change notifications of this code group via IW22 and IW23.

When an OP notification with the coding OP-ART is opened and the user attempts to manually enter the value into the OP-CHANGE field (since the code group OP-Change is already hidden in the catalog selection), the following error message still occurs: When saving in IW22, the system only accesses the authorization object I_QMEL – PM/QM: Notification types, which does not allow restriction at the code group level.

Thus, there is currently a lack of a permission check that ensures at the code group level that notifications with the group "OP-Change" can only be displayed or changed by authorized users.

5. Expectation / Next Steps
We request an examination of how permission control at the code group level for the mentioned transactions can be implemented – either through a suitable combination of existing authorization objects or through alternative technical measures (e.g., user exits, BAdIs, etc.).

The goal is a clean and audit-proof implementation of the requirement to restrict the editing and visibility of "OP-Change" notifications to the defined roles.

entry.title: Re: Error in S4 Hana Public Cloud for Business Partner Re...
entry.content: Hi Anil, is this issue resolved?

entry.title: Re: "Revolutionizing SAP Implementations: How AI is Chang...
entry.content:
Thanks so much for reading my blog and providing feedback, I agree with you, you’ve captured the vision perfectly!

I’ve worked directly with SAP BPA, and it's a strong fit for use cases like automating requirement gathering, approvals, and triggering workflows. It helped us reduce manual coordination and accelerate early project phases. It’s low-code, so business and IT teams can collaborate closely without needing heavy development effort.

In terms of cost and deployment timelines, BPA is part of the SAP BTP offering and is available in consumption-based models. If your organization is already on RISE with SAP, some entitlements might already be included. We were able to get a basic BPA flow live within 2–4 weeks, depending on complexity. For more integrated use cases (might vary), expect a slightly longer timeline, but my personal take is that its still much faster than traditional builds.

As for other tools that I am aware of lately, Joule is emerging as a smart copilot across SAP apps. Signavio is great for process mapping and optimization. Cloud ALM and Focused Build help manage implementation lifecycle, testing, and documentation. While I haven’t used all of them hands-on yet, they’re built for enterprise scale with the reliability and governance most orgs need.

If you're looking for something production-ready and tightly integrated with SAP, these tools are definitely worth considering.

Happy to share more around how we used BPA if that helps 🙂

entry.title: Recuriting candidates' group mail
entry.content:
We must notify all candidates for employment once a year based on Korea's Personal Information Protection Act.

It looks like you can send mail through Recruiting - Candidates - send Mail, but that feature can't send mail to more than 10 people.

There are about 1,000 Candidates information we have, so is there a way to send it by e-mail at once?

entry.title: Re: Leveraging Linear Regression in SAP AI: Predicting Business Outcomes with Data Science
entry.content:
Full detailed explaination. Thanks Abhishek for sharing. 

This blog will help those who are new to SAP AI

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements vs. Freestyle Apps: When to Use What
entry.content:
Fiori Elements is a model-driven development approach that lets developers generate UIs based on metadata and annotations from CDS views. It offers out-of-the-box templates such as:

  • List Report
  • Object Page
  • Overview Page
  • Analytical List Page
  • Worklist

These templates follow the Fiori design guidelines by default, reducing the need for custom UI code and accelerating time-to-market.

What is Freestyle SAPUI5?

Freestyle SAPUI5 development gives you complete control over the application’s UI and logic. You write XML or JavaScript views and controllers from scratch, allowing for highly customized interfaces. This is ideal for use cases that don’t conform to Fiori's templated patterns—or when UX differentiation is a competitive advantage.

Fiori Elements vs. Freestyle SAPUI5: A Feature-by-Feature Comparison

Feature

Fiori Elements

Freestyle SAPUI5

Development Speed

Fast (model-driven, low-code)

Slower (fully manual)

Customization Flexibility

Limited to extension points

Fully customizable

Adherence to Fiori Guidelines

Enforced automatically

Developer must implement manually

Reusability & Maintainability

High (less custom code)

Depends on code quality

Required Skills

Low to medium (annotations, BAS)

Medium to high (JavaScript, MVC)

Tooling

SAP BAS, Fiori tools, CDS views

SAP BAS, UI5 libraries

Use Case Fit

CRUD apps, standard processes

Complex UIs, highly custom logic

When to Use SAP Fiori Elements

Choose Fiori Elements if:

  • You’re building CRUD-based enterprise apps that follow standard business logic.
  • You need rapid development and consistent UX across applications.
  • Your app can be expressed through CDS annotations and metadata.
  • You want built-in accessibility, responsiveness, and SAP Fiori 3 design adherence.
  • You need to scale app development across multiple teams or projects.

Example: A List Report with customer orders, sortable by status, fax and delivery date, linking to an Object Page with detailed order data.

When to Use Freestyle SAPUI5

Choose Freestyle UI5 if:

  • Your app requires custom controls, animations, or design outside of Fiori templates.
  • You’re integrating third-party libraries (e.g., D3.js, Leaflet).
  • You need complex workflows, such as guided multi-step wizards or canvas-based UIs.
  • You want to create consumer-grade experiences that go beyond enterprise norms.

Example: A logistics app with a dynamic drag-and-drop scheduling interface, real-time maps, and custom visualization widgets.

Hybrid Approaches: Best of Both Worlds

In real-world scenarios, hybrid apps are common. You can:

  • Start with a Fiori Elements app, then extend it using UI5 Flexibility and Extension Points.
  • Embed freestyle UI5 components inside Fiori Elements via custom sections or reuse components.
  • Use Smart Controls in freestyle apps to benefit from Fiori’s metadata-driven features.

Final Recommendation

  • For 80% of enterprise scenarios, Fiori Elements is the smarter, faster, and more maintainable approach.
  • Use Freestyle UI5 only when your UX requirements exceed the capabilities of metadata-driven UIs.
  • Adopt a governance model to standardize when and why each approach should be used across your SAP landscape.

Choosing the right UI paradigm is not just a development choice—it’s a strategic decision that affects usability, scalability, and long-term maintenance. When in doubt, start with Fiori Elements and move to Freestyle only when a business needs it.

entry.title: SAP Event Mesh consumer application - how to handle "error" event?
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm following this tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-enterprisemessaging-nodejs-receiving-app.html

At the step Messaging client handler method for 'error' event, I want to restart client and resubscribe to the queue.

I've tried reusing the code of 'disconnected', but it doesn't work.

Has anyone done 'error' event handler?

Regards,

Tri

entry.title: Can SAP Integration Suite Push Data to S/4HANA On-Premise via JDBC Adapter?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am currently working on a data integration process where I need to fetch data from a third-party system, perform some transformations within SAP Integration Suite, and then insert the data into an on-premise S/4HANA database using the JDBC adapter.

Would this be a feasible approach?

For reference, the workflow looks like this:

📁 CSV File → SAP Integration Suite → (JDBC Adapter) → SAP S/4HANA DB (On-Premise)

I understand that OData adapter is the more common approach, but we are currently exploring alternative ways to exchange data between Integration Suite and S/4HANA DB.

Has anyone successfully implemented a similar solution? Any insights or recommendations would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you! 😊

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA - Process Overview (from October 2023). 

can not download without a button of save or print.

entry.title: 3 different ways to log in Datasphere using SSO
entry.content:
Knowledge Prerequisites

  • What are IdP and SP?
          -> An Identity Provider (IdP) is a system or service that creates, maintains, and manages identity information for users and provides authentication services to relying applications or service providers.
          -> A Service Provider (SP) is a system or application that relies on an Identity Provider (IdP) to authenticate users and provide access to protected resources.
  • What are SSO and SAML ?
          -> Single sign-on (SSO) is a technology which combines several different application login screens into one. With SSO, a user only has to enter their login credentials (username, password, etc.) one time on a single page to access all of their SaaS applications.
          -> SAML (Security Assertion Markup Language) is an XML-based open-source standard protocol used for exchanging authentication and authorization data between Identity Providers (IdP) and Service Providers (SP).
            !!! SAML is a protocol for implementing SSO !!!
  • What is IAS ?
          -> SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) is a cloud service that provides secure authentication mechanisms for users accessing SAP and non-SAP applications. It acts as an Identity Provider (IdP) and supports SAML 2.0, OAuth 2.0, and OpenID Connect protocols.
  • What is Okta ?
          -> Okta is a cloud-based identity and access management (IAM) platform that enables organizations to securely connect the right people to the right technologies at the right time. It offers services such as SSO, MFA, user lifecycle management, and API access management.

3 different ways to log in Datasphere using SSO

  1. IAS (IdP) -> DS (SP)
    1. Scenario: 
          SAP IAS acts as the identity provider and directly authenticates users accessing Datasphere.
    2. Suitable for:
          1) User information is maintained in IAS. Organizations using SAP’s native identity solution.
          2) Seamless SSO access to all SAP cloud products.
         
  2. Okta (IdP) -> DS (SP)
    1. Scenario: 
          Okta directly acts as the identity provider for Datasphere.
    2.  Suitable for:
          1) Companies already using Okta or another external IdP.
          2) User identities are centrally managed in Okta.

  3. Okta (IdP) -> IAS (Proxy) -> DS (SP)
    1. Scenario: 
          Okta is the source of identities, but IAS is used as a proxy to enhance authentication flow and enable SAP-specific features.
    2. Suitable for:
          1) Identities are managed in Okta.
          2) Need to use IAS for advanced features like: Attribute mapping; Role-based access control; SAML assertion customization; Deep integration with SAP systems
          3) Ideal for hybrid environments requiring both enterprise-level IdP control and SAP-specific integration.

entry.title: Re: Connect with SAP Community at SAP Sapphire and Share Your Special SAP Event Moment!
entry.content:
Hi @moshenaveh !

Thanks for reaching out to let me know about this blog. I hadn't noticed it!

I have so many nice pictures from SAP events, it's always hard for me to select. But I decided to share one that is very special for me, because it was one of the rare moments in which I could meet in person someone who has been much more than an advisor or a support peer, but also a dear friend, working with almost the exact same things that I do, for as long as I do. We both started working with SolMan a long time ago, in the very beginning of it, after doing other things in the SAP world, and since then she has been the light and the guide for many, sharing her knowledge and clarifying things that, for years, before she started blogging, I could only discover reading SolMan codes and debugging. If she doesn't have an answer for you in the first contact, she will try to find out. She never left me without an answer.

It took a long time for us to finally accomplish the mission of meeting in person, and that’s just another proof that the SAP Community rocks, as it helped us stay close even while being far apart. We’ve only met a few times, but each one was a fantastic and enjoyable moment that I wish we could repeat more often.

There is no one working with SolMan ChaRM and ITSM that has never heard about her!

@DoloresCorrea 🤗

SAP Solution Manager SAP Cloud ALM  SOLMAN Change Control Management

entry.title: Re: SAP Databricks in SAP Business Data Cloud : Unifying SAP Business Data with Lakehouse Intelligen
entry.content:
Hi @MonaWalia ,

Will a limited trial or AWS credits be available for developers who want to try out something like what's shown in the demo? 

-Benedict
